turns.content: bro quick question how far in advance do you gotta get fitted for a suit like a real suit not just goin to mens wearhouse or whatever

For a properly tailored suit, you generally want to start the process at least 2-3 months before the event. That gives time for the initial fitting, any fabric ordering, and then 1-2 rounds of alterations. If you're going with a custom suit, some places need even more lead time — like 3-4 months. What's the occasion?
my sister bridget is gettin married in june and im a groomsman so I gotta look like I actually know how to dress myself for once lol

That's awesome, congrats to your sister! Being a groomsman is a big deal. If the wedding is in June, you've got a good amount of time still. Usually the groom or the bride will coordinate what the groomsmen are wearing — do you know yet if they've picked out specific suits or a color scheme?

yeah bridgets fiance mike is handlin all that hes got some vision for it I guess navy blue suits or somethin. the weddings june 14th so I figured I still got time but my mom is already on my case about it like every single day bro

Ha, moms and weddings — that's a universal thing. June 14th gives you plenty of runway. If Mike has navy blue suits picked out, he'll probably coordinate a specific shop or designer for all the groomsmen so everyone matches. I'd just check with him on where he wants everyone to go and when. You're fine on timing though — you could even get fitted in March or April and be totally good.

thats what I told my mom im like relax its 7 months away but you know how she is with this stuff shes been goin crazy since bridget got engaged. the whole house is like wedding central every time I go over there

That's totally normal — for a lot of moms the daughter's wedding is THE event. Seven months out and she's probably deep into centerpiece options and seating charts already. Your job as a groomsman is pretty easy honestly — show up when they tell you, get the suit, and don't lose the ring if you're holding it.
lol nah thats the best man mikes brother is doin that thank god I would be so stressed holdin onto a ring all day id probably drop it down a drain or somethin

Smart move keeping you away from ring duty. So your main responsibilities are probably the suit fitting, showing up to the rehearsal dinner, maybe helping plan the bachelor party, and walking down the aisle. Have they started talking about a bachelor party for Mike yet?
yeah mikes brother is doin that too I think theyre talkin about goin to foxwoods for a weekend which would be sick I been wantin to go there for a while

Foxwoods is a great bachelor party spot — you've got the casino, shows, restaurants, and it's not too far from Boston. A weekend trip there would be a blast. That's usually a good time to bond with the groom's side too if you don't know all of Mike's friends yet.

yeah I know a couple of em but not all of em. honestly im just happy for bridget shes been with mike for like 4 years and hes a solid dude treats her right and everythin. my dad actually likes him too which is like impossible to get my dad to like anybody

When the dad approves, you know it's legit. Four years together is a great foundation too — they clearly know what they're getting into. Sounds like your sister found a good one. And hey, gaining a brother-in-law you actually get along with is a big win.

for real bro mike comes to the bruins games with us and everythin hes basically already part of the crew. alright I gotta bounce im headin to the shop to help my dad close up but yeah ill talk to mike about the suit stuff soon so my mom chills out

Sounds like a plan. Just shoot Mike a text about the suit situation and relay that back to your mom — should buy you some peace for at least a week or two. Congrats again on Bridget's wedding, that's going to be a great time. Have a good night!
